Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria, suggesting possible diabetic nephropathy or acute metabolic decompensation. Low urine specific gravity may reflect impaired concentrating ability. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but no evidence of infection. Low serum globulin and mildly low AST are noted. Recommend further evaluation of renal function, glycemic control, and possible underlying metabolic or renal pathology.
